events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Richard Starkey, known as Ringo Starr, was born on July 7, 1940, and achieved international fame as the drummer for The Beatles.

angles.name: Ringo's Beat
angles.copy: Happy 86th Birthday to the legendary Ringo Starr! The man who kept the beat for the Fab Four, what's your favourite Ringo moment or track?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Sir Brian Harold May, born on July 19, 1947, is an English musician, best known as the lead guitarist and backing vocalist of the rock band Queen.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Sending huge birthday wishes to the iconic Brian May, turning 79 today! From Queen anthems to astrophysics, what's your favourite Brian May guitar solo?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, born on July 26, 1943, is an English musician, songwriter, and film producer, widely known as the lead singer and founder member of The Rolling Stones.

angles.name: Rock & Roll Legend
angles.copy: Happy 83rd Birthday to the one and only Mick Jagger! Six decades of rock and still moving like Jagger. What's your top Rolling Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: MTV, the first 24-hour music video channel, launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' being the first music video aired.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched and changed music forever! Remember staying up late just to catch your favourite music videos? What was the first video you remember seeing?

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut studio album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which quickly became a critical and commercial success, defining the Britpop era.

angles.name: Definitely Iconic
angles.copy: It's 32 years since Oasis dropped their seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe'. An instant classic that defined a generation. What's your favourite track from the album?

events.title: Woodstock Festival (1969)
events.blurb: The Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, took place from August 15-18, 1969, attracting over 400,000 people for '3 Days of Peace & Music'.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: 57 years ago today, Woodstock began! Three days of peace, music, and mud that became a cultural phenomenon. If you could have been there, which artist would you most want to see?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's Death
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977, at the age of 42. He would have been 91.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Remembering the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, who passed away on this day in 1977. His legacy continues to inspire generations. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles' eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', was released on September 26, 1969, featuring iconic tracks and its famous zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Crossing The Road
angles.copy: 57 years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover is one of the most famous in history! What's your favourite track from this masterpiece?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Christine Ellen Hynde, born on September 7, 1951, is an American-British singer, songwriter, and musician, best known as a founding member of The Pretenders.

angles.name: Pretenders Icon
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! Lead singer of The Pretenders and a true rock icon. What's your favourite track from Chrissie?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, born on September 21, 1972, is an English singer and songwriter, best known as the lead singer and co-founder of Oasis.

angles.name: Mad For It
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! The voice of a generation and still rocking. What's your favourite Oasis anthem he sang?

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic song 'Imagine' was released as a single on October 11, 1971, from his album of the same name.

angles.name: Imagine All The People
angles.copy: 55 years ago, John Lennon gave the world 'Imagine', a timeless anthem of peace and hope. What does this song mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, and it became available on November 10, 2001, revolutionising how people listened to music.

angles.name: Pocket Full of Tunes
angles.copy: Remember life before the iPod? 25 years ago, Apple dropped the original iPod, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket'. What was the first album you loaded onto yours?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day celebrates the release of the first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', on October 5, 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating 64 years since Dr. No premiered. Who's your favourite Bond, and what's the best Bond theme tune of all time?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Winston Ono Lennon, born on October 9, 1940, was an English musician, singer, and songwriter who gained worldwide fame as a co-founder of The Beatles.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering the visionary John Lennon, who would have been 86 today. His music and message continue to resonate globally. What's your favourite John Lennon song?
